properties.acceptance: all RED tests green, 324 passing, F1/F4/F6 closed

properties.text: Spec 029 RED-GREEN cycle complete: 4 new tests for intent_bootstrap (incl. isomorphism MCP==CLI), 4 for agency_welcome (incl. token-budget invariant 1KB), 5 for agency_install (incl. CLAUDE.md marker idempotency), 1 for SERVES-guard message, 2 for reflect-scope enum disclosure. 324 passed, 1 skipped (non-writable target test skips as root).

OQ-3 (welcome token budget) hit during impl: full {capability: [verb,...]} payload landed at 1.2 KB on the live registry (Jules alone has 21 verbs). Resolved by switching to names-only list + agents fetch verbs via capability_plugin_help on demand. Net token saving ~800 B per first contact vs. full-map welcome.

Spec-panel discussion mode caught 6 gaps the in-skill review missed: error-contract pinning (Wiegers/Nygard), no-Invocation invariant for bootstrap (Crispin), additive-params evolution doctrine (Newman), A1/A2/A3 actor enumeration (Cockburn), Given/When/Then examples for install (Adzic), failure-modes section (Nygard). Multi-expert pass was net-positive.
F2 (JULES_API_KEY stale-cache) and F3 (effect-verb idempotency) deferred — orthogonal touch points (Jules client init vs. sandbox transaction model). Tracked as future Spec 030/031.

Plugin-development capability gap noticed during impl: lint_capability runs in warn mode for legacy capabilities (no agency-scaffold marker), so the engine.py substrate tool additions are NOT linted. Substrate tools are not capabilities — they have no source file with a scaffold marker. Acceptable but worth tracking.

The CLAUDE.md marker writer keeps user content outside markers intact (test_agency_install_preserves_existing_claude_md) and replaces between markers on re-run (test_agency_install_replaces_marker_block_on_rerun). The contract: agent owns content between markers, user owns everything outside, never modify outside markers — Janet Gregory three-amigos artifact.

properties.deliverable: F2 error msg, agency_doctor, state-aware welcome, F5 docs
properties.purpose: Spec 030 - JULES key + doctor + stateful welcome

properties.text: Spec 030 RED-GREEN complete: F2 error message rewrite + agency_doctor substrate tool + stateful agency_welcome + AGENTS.md F5 doc fix. 333 passing (was 324 after Spec 029).
agency_doctor security invariant: JULES_API_KEY VALUE never in report - test_doctor_does_not_leak_jules_key proves it via leak-canary string. Doctor returns set/missing only.

Stateful welcome subsumes empty-graph search hint via canonical onboarding tool. Welcome on fresh graph -> next leads with install + bootstrap; on populated -> next leads with search + provenance, surfaces last_intent. Resumption-aware first-call.

F2 root cause was substitution timing, not lazy reading: _api_key() does read os.environ each call (lazy), but the user_config substitution happens at MCP server START - the inherited env is fixed for that process. Fix is documentation, not code. New error message names user_config + reload path.

Loop 2 ran without invoking sc:sc-spec-panel - Loop 1 established the pattern and the spec was small enough that the in-skill self-review caught the gaps (OQ-1 DB-write side effect, OQ-2 wrapping FastMCP search). Saved tokens of expert dialogue without sacrificing rigor.

engine.build_mcp now hosts 5 substrate tools: lifecycle_gate, memory_graph_provenance, intent_bootstrap (Spec 029), agency_install (Spec 029), agency_welcome (Spec 029, made stateful in 030), agency_doctor (Spec 030). All flat-named; per Fowler this is the threshold for naming convention - track CORE.md update.

properties.acceptance: spec.md updated, depends_on flipped, file paths re-routed, lint+budget invariants added
properties.deliverable: agency/capabilities/novel/ folder-form + scaffold-first discipline + Spec 023 token budget

properties.text: Spec 010 updated 2026-05-31: elevated novel from examples/ to agency/capabilities/novel/ (folder-form, auto-discovered per Spec 016 Phase 3 commit 8a5a45d). KP repo is canonical user-facing application, not third-party domain proof.

depends_on flipped from [001, 002, 003] to [001, 016]. Spec 002 (Driver registry) additive - novel uses no external drivers (Dramatica + NCP are vendored static JSON). Spec 003 (typed Skill/Phase) refactor that keeps storing dicts - work-concept skill authoring unchanged.

develop.scaffold_capability(name=novel, kind=heavy) is the canonical bootstrap. The agency-scaffold v1 marker flips lint_capability to block mode by construction. Hand-editing starts AFTER scaffold lints clean - non-negotiable per Spec 024.

Per-verb docstring brief must be <=120 chars / <=20 cl100k tokens (Spec 023 adaptive-disclosure gate). lint_capability enforces in block mode. Brief surfaces in search + capability_plugin_help + FastMCP tools/list.

jsonschema dep moves from requirements-examples.txt to a [novel] optional-dependencies extra in pyproject.toml. Core engine never imports jsonschema; _ncp.py does lazy import inside ncp_validate. Core dep-purity intact.

Implementation order remains 7 loops post-PR-0 (scaffold + data): dramatica_lookup, ncp_validate, OntologyExtension + scaffold (act/effect), coherence_check, pre_drafting_gate, conceptualize + work-concept skill, end-to-end docs/examples/author_a_novel.py. Each loop = one PR per Spec 016 Phase 5 conftest fixtures discipline.
